questions.question: What was your overall impression of the product?
questions.answer: My overall impression is...rough. It felt incomplete. I landed on a tasks page that acted like I was logged in already, even though I wasn't. Then, after signing out, there was no clear way to actually register, which is what I was trying to do. Very frustrating.
questions.insight: The product needs a clear and functional registration process and a fix for the false 'logged in' state.

questions.question: What was the most confusing part?
questions.answer: Definitely the 'Welcome back!' and 'Successfully logged in' message right when I landed on the page, even though I hadn't even registered yet. That just made no sense and immediately put me off.
questions.insight: The application needs to accurately reflect the user's login status.

questions.question: What was the most delightful part?
questions.answer: Honestly, there wasn't anything that I'd call 'delightful'. The task view options *seemed* comprehensive, but I couldn't actually use any of them without being able to register and log in.
questions.insight: The product needs to have its core functionality working for users to find any part of it delightful.

questions.question: How would you feel if you could no longer use this product?
questions.insight: The product in its current state does not provide value and therefore will not be missed.

questions.question: Would you use this again tomorrow? Why or why not?
questions.answer: Probably not. Why would I? I couldn't even register. Until they fix the core registration issue, it's unusable.
questions.insight: The product needs to enable core task completion for user retention.

questions.question: Who specifically would you recommend this to? What would you tell them?
questions.answer: I wouldn't recommend this to anyone in its current state. I'd tell them to stay far, far away until they get the basics working.
questions.insight: The product is not in a state ready for recommendation. Core issues need to be resolved.

questions.question: What would you pay for this? What do you currently pay for alternatives?
questions.answer: Right now? Zero. I wouldn't pay anything for a product I can't even use. I currently pay $15/month for Asana, which, you know, actually lets me create an account and manage tasks.
questions.insight: The product's value needs to be significantly improved before it becomes worth paying for.

questions.question: What would need to change for you to commit to using this regularly?
questions.answer: The most important thing is a working registration process. After that, I'd need to actually be able to log in and create tasks before I could even consider using it regularly. And maybe a less overwhelming initial UI.
questions.insight: The product needs a functional registration/login and task creation process, as well as simplified UX.

questions.question: What's missing that you expected to find?
questions.answer: A registration form! It's the first thing any new user needs. It was bizarre that it was completely absent.
questions.insight: The application lacks the most basic functionality.

questions.question: How does this compare to [competitor/alternative]?
questions.answer: Compared to Asana, this is... not even in the same ballpark. Asana is polished, intuitive, and actually lets me *do* things. This is just broken.
questions.insight: The product lags far behind established alternatives in terms of functionality and usability.

questions.question: What was your "aha moment" if any?
questions.answer: No aha moment. Just a lot of head-scratching and frustration.
questions.insight: No element of discovery or enjoyment existed in the application.

questions.question: On a scale of 1-10, how likely are you to recommend this?
questions.answer: 1. Maybe even a 0. Honestly, it's that bad right now.
questions.insight: The current product experience is detrimental to building positive word of mouth.

referral_specificity: No one. Avoid until core issues are resolved.
